We saved health care once before, team.

Remember when the House bill failed and everyone said repeal was DOA... and then the GOP made a new push and got it passed within 48 hours?

They could do it again. They just need our complacency -- especially now that John McCain has signaled he's willing to support the new repeal bill.

name: The White House
subject: President Trump Heads to the 72nd United Nations General Assembly
body:
1600 Daily
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=0e14f8733c5e3ae248071221f9b8785f3182691583f5b29f8c0d7aa9739f98daddeb6add98560eb62b177306ecccf86c7e4a29c08b677c00

President Donald J. Trump will be participating in the 72nd United Nations General Assembly this week. The President will meet with world leaders to discuss the many challenges facing the globe, as well as how the United States can continue to join with others to promote peace and prosperity and uphold sovereignty and accountability.

-------------

President Trump Leads the United States’ Delegation to the 72nd United Nations General Assembly

President Trump will lead the United States’ delegation to the 72nd United Nations General Assembly in New York City this week, where he will participate in approximately 20 meetings with world leaders and other events.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=0e14f8733c5e3ae2ec14a9c897128e21014e9d18b7ac44cab74ba855d319739c8c22abb486ae7e3c9ff978e6664dadeccff952dc96acb872
Read more

The United States Air Force is 70 Years Strong

President Trump and First Lady Melania Trump paid a visit to Joint Base Andrews in Maryland on Friday to commemorate the seventieth birthday of the United States Air Force. The President delivered remarks to Service members and their families saying, “Americans have trusted in the United States Air Force because we know you will never flee, you will never yield, and you will never fail.”

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=0e14f8733c5e3ae238e95af32d5fbc98eed6a0d546a91bb69fe07af9877e1921139aaaa23891508baca470bb0f857e2fce67a7ebbb1747f1
Watch the video

Frank's Dream Comes True

11-year-old Frank “FX” Giaccio wrote to President Trump earlier this year to offer his services in mowing the White House lawn free of charge. On Friday, Frank's dream came true. As Frank mowed the Rose Garden lawn, President Trump met him outside to applaud his work ethic and lawn care expertise. Frank then had the opportunity to join President Trump in the Oval Office and shortly after was pinned a Junior Park Ranger in the Rose Garden.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=0e14f8733c5e3ae24d518d219fac58774c45713109a978eea0f20448a7189e4c1052825ceb816478c3ca30d8af2e630ac0464a2ba1c04f94
Watch the video

Photo of the Day

First Lady Melania Trump delivers remarks to military personnel and families at Joint Base Andrews | September 15, 2017 (Official White House Photo by Andrea Hanks)

POTUS and VP TODAY

Today, President Trump will arrive at the headquarters of the United Nations in New York City and deliver remarks on reforming the UN through changes in management, security, and development. This afternoon, he will participate in bilateral meetings with leaders from Israel and France followed by a working dinner with Latin American leaders.

COMING UP

President Trump will be at the United Nations General Assembly most of this week. He will deliver an address to the 72nd United General Assembly, and participate in bilateral meetings with numerous world leaders.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=0e14f8733c5e3ae24103320492461ac77ea532e1202ba7dbd1eeed6486978f1037423e8acf94927ba3ad526282323f906d5afff7804daa75
Privacy Policy |

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=0e14f8733c5e3ae20a49b6432e95427283a0d0b4be313a09977421c71d3d3b97b1e0a3876b5877aace8fe6ec2c6b1d7ddab9be745799e292
Contact the White House |

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/unsub_center.aspx?qs=d453c42a6560d1d4f390543ef179f8facbff0886ea06464387feca343dd56bba9dcc30b3d57c50a42df0b0499c6c46ace153bfc3ce1ead9e

name: Tom Perez
subject: Tell Republicans: stop trying to take away our health care (add your name)
body:
Say you'll protect affordable health care.
---
**Add your name: tell Senate Republicans you'll fight to protect affordable health care.

** **[I'M IN](http://my.democrats. "http://my.democrats.org/Stop-Health-Care-Repeal1?keycode=&firstname=Derek&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=20112&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=1+-+IM+IN&utm_campaign=em_20170918_tp_v1s1&source=em_20170918_tp_v1s1")**

Derek, Republicans are ignoring the will of the American people and trying to take away our health care -- again.

Republican senators have proposed new ACA repeal legislation that will sabotage our health care system, gut Medicaid, increase the costs you and I pay, and take health care away from millions of Americans.

I'm going to keep saying this over and over until Republicans get it: health care is a right, not a privilege. Americans shouldn't have to choose between putting food on the table and getting the care they need.

It's past time for Republicans to join Democrats in fixing our health care system, instead of staying on this dead-end path of attempting to dismantle Obamacare.

**Add your name -- tell congressional Republicans to stop trying to take away our health care:**

**[I'M IN](http://my.democrats. "http://my.democrats.org/Stop-Health-Care-Repeal1?keycode=&firstname=Derek&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=20112&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=2+-+IM+IN&utm_campaign=em_20170918_tp_v1s1&source=em_20170918_tp_v1s1")**
Thanks,

Tom

Chair

disclaimer_text: Paid for and Authorized by Debbie Wasserman Schultz for Congress This email was sent to [dpwillis67@gmail
body:
| |

| |
---
| We have another opponent friends. We're more than a year out from election day and already another opponent has thrown his hat into the ring. Instead of talking about the issues that matter to Florida families or what policies he would support, he held a rally to spend time attacking me. They can try and attack my record as much as they want. **I will continue doing what I have been** \-- _actually working for the constituents of this district and putting their best interests first._ I have no doubt that they will continue to attack me and try to distract from the results that I've achieved on behalf of South Florida. We need to grow our grassroots team and make sure that they don't set the narrative for this race. [_**My opponents are getting an early start so we can 't afford to fall behind. Can I count on you to rush in $5 or $10?**_](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113e3f/622b7b5b/5fd3c00/307bd6ee/3033364631/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/dwse170915?refcode=dwse170915_m&recurring=true&amount=5") While my opponents have been doing a lot of talking, I've been getting to work. I've authored legislation that would improve health care access for our veterans and provide housing to survivors of domestic violence. I've co-sponsored bills that would protect our voting rights and improve workers' rights. I know my opponents will come after me in a lot of ways. They always do. But while they are busy playing politics, I'll be busy doing what I always do -- _fighting for South Florida families and getting results._ Having a strong grassroots team means we can focus more on getting results and less on campaigning. [_**The key to victory is to be prepared and get started early. Will you help us stand strong by making a $5 or $10 contribution?**_](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113e3f/622b7b5b/5fd3c00/307bd6ee/3033364631/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/dwse170915?refcode=dwse170915_m&recurring=true&amount=5") Thank you, Debbie Sent from my iPad |

name: Jill Vogel
subject: Free tuition for those here illegally?
disclaimer_text: Paid for and Authorized by Vogel for Virginia45 North Hill Drive, Suite 100Warrenton, VA 20186
body:
---
| | | | | |
---
| | |
---
| Dear Peter,
Should those here illegally be eligible for in-state tuition at our public colleges and universities? What about free tuition, as my opponent has proposed?
Over the last decade, college costs have increased three times faster than inflation, leaving too many families struggling to afford higher education.
Every public institution has a limited number of slots for in-state students. They should not be denying access to those here legally for those limited slots by giving them to those here illegally, at the expense of the taxpayers who support our higher education system.
As for "free tuition," [like my opponent promised to a Bernie Sanders group](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001aFcFg5gSUNmOntZGvyVKpwWqaT7ALHKg4D0rAzGcPUYf4eEcm14-t9eQbOXgzwyMX1TKTybtM5DvWhpZnUW7ICYeNUVUKFuky83Da-kgoSSprMbUtROGzwfwKWLGqPsGWYhdmpBqgqy1c4u7NPZAnr54kGZ4o8g-GoWMeEsqRqXm-TB3-396y96J1DymrDvrHGkd5NthqWyCGHH2w1MeC-MYbG5fJjfhSwTSoyWIS2E=&c=Z8MS-6X6e_8v5TmfnY-rGBIZY72fS5Y-27wgC0CoXZDAvi2fT0cSRw==&ch=mBEXnSvqO0Q_52lG0ODgnWrfV8IH4JFxT3PuAriBPxHkFLXd1r2viw==) who [endorsed him in the primary](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001aFcFg5gSUNmOntZGvyVKpwWqaT7ALHKg4D0rAzGcPUYf4eEcm14-t9eQbOXgzwyMb47mL-jV6RPXAG9PBSveG4_CHmRGZqNMVm6HyZu35INGbTGypmWDWu5tK7ZZou3NFsaTMWBfZH7xzoUhK4Y_POi-IfL_NXuaA5Oe2E2B9zsnEavrGprd2cCp1De8c0e5WMyRkrsC1yt_3Aj9T9EQfU_HChUC00vJxJe-KjcGYAGajKYE5ZrJOS6ys2ZYx4XSsstzgQn0ugw74-96uQUOq3UGsHWAWf0_c_AVJVZjTLFkCE3U2Ayamec62hgURwfc&c=Z8MS-6X6e_8v5TmfnY-rGBIZY72fS5Y-27wgC0CoXZDAvi2fT0cSRw==&ch=mBEXnSvqO0Q_52lG0ODgnWrfV8IH4JFxT3PuAriBPxHkFLXd1r2viw==) \- we cannot afford it. Here is what he said:
---
|
---
| He did not say how he would pay for it.
The issue of immigration is one place where my opponent and I differ.
I support the rule of law and believe we must follow the laws on the books. If we do not like those laws, we can change them, but we cannot pick and choose which laws to follow.
I oppose in-state tuition for those here illegally. I voted to ban sanctuary cities, and I support using e-Verify to level the playing field for businesses who should only be hiring legal workers.
By contrast, my opponent supports in-state tuition for those here illegally, does not support an e-Verify requirement, and also supports the establishment of sanctuary cities.
At a candidate forum, he pledged to, "fight any attempt to have the federal government make us comply with the Trump Administration's policies around immigration and deportation, specifically."
Virginia needs a Lt. Governor who will uphold the rule of law and ensure access to opportunity for those who followed the rules. Virginia needs leaders with real-world experience writing budgets, who understand that we must pay for our proposals.
If you share my belief in the rule of law, please help us get our message out in the last 50 days of the campaign by [making a contribution of $1000, $500, $100, or even $50](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001aFcFg5gSUNmOntZGvyVKpwWqaT7ALHKg4D0rAzGcPUYf4eEcm14-t-r65I2PdBu-Mw6smE4ERww-ObDGH1fNOBP_uN6v920H4bcO2AZbMp9y5dwQj3RNMtzBSbLu6bNeaYP_1H1yywNU1oXS1vxa2OhqSHCaNth1tVifgABb-NQ=&c=Z8MS-6X6e_8v5TmfnY-rGBIZY72fS5Y-27wgC0CoXZDAvi2fT0cSRw==&ch=mBEXnSvqO0Q_52lG0ODgnWrfV8IH4JFxT3PuAriBPxHkFLXd1r2viw==).

Sincerely,
---
|
---
| Jill Vogel

name: Jay Inslee
subject: ONE QUESTION SURVEY: Climate change
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jay Inslee for Washington
body:
After Harvey and Irma, is it time to take action on climate change?

| [](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=3&ms=MTE5NDgwMjAS1&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTI0MTUzMzUxMAS2&mt=1&rt=0) Friend, This past month, we have watched as extreme weather devastated parts of our country -- from Texas to Florida, its impact has been tragic. And I'm committed to making sure these horrific disasters don't happen as often as they do now. We know that **denying climate change gambles with our lives and the lives of our children.** [**Should we take action on climate action _now_? name: Tim Kaine
subject: We need your help to keep Virginia blue
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kaine for Virginia
body:
---
| **Ralph Northam and I are committed to continuing to serve you, here in the Commonwealth and in the Capitol. But we need your support -- donate to his campaign for governor today to help make sure we elect Ralph in November.**

**[DONATE](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=9Pig1XIrYriiqKLqEo2z%2Fw1KdZM0ImjB)**
---
Derek,

When I first ran for state office in 2001, Virginia was a very different state politically. Every statewide official was a Republican and we had not supported a Democratic presidential candidate since 1964.

Today Democrats hold all statewide offices and we have gone blue in the last 3 presidential races. That only happened because of your hard work. Virginia is a critical battleground state nationally, and we have a real fight on our hands here in our state races this year.

As we face leadership in Washington D.C. that tests our core values, we have the opportunity to take a stand and set a real example as the eyes of the nation turn to Virginia. We need to make sure Ralph Northam is elected our next governor in November \-- and that we win this fight.

Ralph's opponent, Ed Gillespie, wants to take Virginia backwards, and he has millions of special interest dollars pouring into the race supporting him. We can't let them win. We have to continue our progress. As I'm working in the Senate to strengthen health care and education, we need Ralph in Richmond doing the same. His election is less than two months away. **[Can you make a donation today to support him?](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=Iz1yH7rAQybB4HCwhXoBPA1KdZM0ImjB)**

The fact is, keeping our economy flourishing and giving a fair shot to all Virginians is a big job. None of us can do it alone. I'm so grateful for your continued support, and I'm going to need it to win my Senate race next year. But Ralph needs it, too \-- and he needs it now. We absolutely can't let Ed Gillespie bring President Trump's agenda to Richmond.

We need a governor who is honest and committed to progress, no matter what Trump is doing in the White House. As a doctor and a veteran, Ralph has spent his life serving others -- and as governor, I know he will serve us well.

**[Will you help make sure he's ready to win in November by making a contribution to his campaign?](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=NyEHJzSC%2F%2Fbj2lK9inf64g1KdZM0ImjB)**

I've known Ralph for a long time, and he and I are both ready to keep serving you. I can't thank you enough for your support.

Thank you,

Tim | | [**DONATE**](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=9Pig1XIrYrgOTKPRd8pDPw1KdZM0ImjB)
---
| In connection with this mailer, we are soliciting only donations of up to $2,700 from individuals and up to $5,000 from multi-candidate political committees. We are not soliciting donations in any amount from corporations, labor organizations, foreign nationals, federal contractors, or national banks in connection with this mailer.

Paid for by Kaine for Virginia
---
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com.
If you no longer wish to receive updates from our campaign, please [unsubscribe](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=5Qa9FJNZGOCMYOA0xo%2BbcA1KdZM0ImjB).
---

The political world has their eyes on Indiana's Senate race. name: Lucinda Guinn, EMILY's List
subject: These elected officials have huge power over our elections
body:
We elect pro-choice Democratic women who will fight for women and families. | [ ](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c60f7/24c69fd9/9ca87e22/189aa457/1864158275/VEsH/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/donate-to-emily?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=1+-+%0D%0A++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++&utm_campaign=WQEFE932N_digital_email_20170918_OnNoticeWhySOS_inact&source=WQEFE932N_digital_email_20170918_OnNoticeWhySOS_inact")
---

Peter,

We need to talk about secretaries of state.

Not the ones who serve the president, but the dozens across the country who are responsible for making sure our elections are transparent and fair.

Too often, we only think about them when something's gone wrong. Think back to Florida in 2000, when the Republican secretary of state led a bitterly contested ballot-counting initiative in a tight race that ultimately settled the election in favor of... George W. Bush.

We can't take these small but powerful roles for granted. That's why we've put three terrible Republican secretaries of state "On Notice" -- we're aiming to replace them with pro-choice Democratic women who will protect our elections.

**[Donate $3 now to help recruit and support pro-choice Democratic women to run for secretary of state positions across the country.](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c60f7/24c69fd9/9ca87e22/189aa450/1864158275/VEsE/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20170918_email_OnNoticeWhySOS?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=2+-+Donate+3+now+to+help+recruit+and+support&utm_campaign=WQEFE932N_digital_email_20170918_OnNoticeWhySOS_inact&source=WQEFE932N_digital_email_20170918_OnNoticeWhySOS_inact")**

Here's a bit about the three secretaries of state we've put On Notice:
**Paul Pate (IA)** pushed a voter ID law that makes voting more difficult, especially for low-income, elderly, and African American voters.

**Wayne Williams (CO)** turned over voter-identifying materials to Trump's sham voter fraud commission. He also supported forcing women to undergo mandatory ultrasounds prior to an abortion.

As a state lawmaker, **Michele Reagan (AZ)** voted to allow the state to search abortion clinics without a warrant, supported defunding Planned Parenthood, and supported allowing companies to deny women contraception coverage. It's dangerous to trust elections to someone so extreme.
Secretaries of state have the power to influence voter turnout, protect sensitive voter data, and enforce election laws. And just like in 2000, they can potentially affect the outcome of close races.

Republicans have been using these offices for too long to suppress voters and tilt the electoral playing field to boost their own party. So we're making defeating them a priority.

**Donate $3 now to oust On Notice Republican secretaries of state and elect pro-choice Democratic women nationwide:**

**[https://secure.emilyslist.org/Oust-Republican-SOS](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c60f7/24c69fd9/9ca87e22/189aa451/1864158275/VEsF/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20170918_email_OnNoticeWhySOS?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=3+-+httpssecureemilyslistorgOustRepublicanSO&utm_campaign=WQEFE932N_digital_email_20170918_OnNoticeWhySOS_inact&source=WQEFE932N_digital_email_20170918_OnNoticeWhySOS_inact")**

Thanks,

Lucinda Guinn
Vice President of Campaigns, EMILY's List

[](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c60f7/24c69fd9/9ca87e22/189aa452/1864158275/VEsC/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20170918_email_OnNoticeWhySOS?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=4&utm_campaign=WQEFE932N_digital_email_20170918_OnNoticeWhySOS_inact&source=WQEFE932N_digital_email_20170918_OnNoticeWhySOS_inact")

name: Roy Cooper
subject: ONE QUESTION SURVEY: School supplies
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Cooper for North Carolina
body:
With school back in session across North Carolina, I've been thinking a lot about our teachers. I've been thinking about how hard they work and how much they sacrifice.
|
[](http://act.roycooper.com/page/m/12032cf/2fde7c66/45fbe26a/15694a39/2103191285/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVkZUVUZKVENVbCI6ImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIiwiSlNWYVNWQWxKUT09IjoiMjg0NTcifQ==/ "http://act.roycooper.com/page/s/school-supplies-survey?utm_campaign=em17_170918_school_adv_gq_ful__RC&utm_medium=email&utm_source=Cooper&source=em17_170918_school_adv_gq_ful__RC?&firstname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=28457&utm_content=1")
---
Friend,

With school back in session across North Carolina, I've been thinking a lot about our teachers. I've been thinking about how hard they work and how much they sacrifice.

We know that teachers are reaching into their own pockets to cover the cost of supplies for their students and classrooms -- studies show the average teacher spends $500 a year on school supplies. That's why, when I announced my budget in February, I included a school supply stipend for teachers.

**[Unfortunately, that stipend wasn't included in the final budget the GOP legislature passed. But I want to hear from you. Do you agree we should do more for our teachers in the budget?](http://act.roycooper.com/page/m/12032cf/2fde7c66/45fbe26a/15694a38/2103191285/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVkZUVUZKVENVbCI6ImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIiwiSlNWYVNWQWxKUT09IjoiMjg0NTcifQ==/ "http://act.roycooper.com/page/s/school-supplies-survey?utm_campaign=em17_170918_school_adv_gq_ful__RC&utm_medium=email&utm_source=Cooper&source=em17_170918_school_adv_gq_ful__RC?&firstname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=28457&utm_content=2+-+Unfortunately+that+stipend+wasnt+include")**

In early August, I launched the Governor's School Supply Drive to help offset what our teachers are paying for out of their own pockets. Last week, I was able to visit schools to deliver the supplies and also spend time listening to our teachers.

One of the things I heard over and over again was about how our teachers are having to cover the cost of even the most basic school supplies like paper and pencils.

It's my hope that one day a drive like this won't be necessary and that in the future our legislature will step up and help our teachers more.

**[What do you think? Should the school supply stipend have been included in the GOP legislature's final budget?](http://act.roycooper.com/page/m/12032cf/2fde7c66/45fbe26a/15694a3b/2103191285/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVkZUVUZKVENVbCI6ImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIiwiSlNWYVNWQWxKUT09IjoiMjg0NTcifQ==/ "http://act.roycooper.com/page/s/school-supplies-survey?utm_campaign=em17_170918_school_adv_gq_ful__RC&utm_medium=email&utm_source=Cooper&source=em17_170918_school_adv_gq_ful__RC?&firstname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=28457&utm_content=3+-+What+do+you+think+Should+the+school+supp")**

Thanks for all you continue to do,

Roy | | [**TAKE THE SURVEY ->**](http://act.roycooper.com/page/m/12032cf/2fde7c66/45fbe26a/15694a3a/2103191285/VEsC/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVkZUVUZKVENVbCI6ImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIiwiSlNWYVNWQWxKUT09IjoiMjg0NTcifQ==/ "http://act.roycooper.com/page/s/school-supplies-survey?utm_campaign=em17_170918_school_adv_gq_ful__RC&utm_medium=email&utm_source=Cooper&source=em17_170918_school_adv_gq_ful__RC?&firstname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=28457&utm_content=4+-+TAKE+THE+SURVEY+rarr")
